feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: CALM: How is the Upgrade Roadmap used in parallel with an Implementation Roadmap?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Upgrade Roadmap states:

"The roadmap can be used as a standalone mini project for a customer who is already live with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, without ongoing projects. It can also be used in parallel, or have tasks added to an ongoing new implementation or continuous improvement project."

My understanding is therefore that SAP explicitly supports using upgrade activities during an ongoing implementation project.

However, in SAP Cloud ALM, a project appears to be assigned to a single SAP Activate roadmap.

My question is:

How is SAP's recommendation implemented in SAP Cloud ALM when upgrade activities need to be managed in parallel with an ongoing implementation?

For example:

  • Do you create a second SAP Cloud ALM project based on the Upgrade Roadmap?
  • Do you manually add the upgrade tasks to the implementation project?
  • Is there any way to consume both roadmaps within the same project?

I would appreciate any guidance from SAP or implementation partners who have managed Public Cloud projects across mandatory SAP release upgrades.

Thank you.

entry.title: Supplier evaluation by inspection lot
entry.content:
Hello, 

How does the supplier evaluation works via inspection lots, the criterion is grayed out and I cannot modify it 

how its calculated?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Screen Personas on mobgui for SAP Public Cloud not working on Ivanti Velocity Web Browser
entry.content:
Hi all,

My current customer is using the Ivanti Velocity Web Browser on his Zebra device to run the RF Mobgui in SAP Public Cloud. As some adjustments needed to be done, screen personas was used to make the changes. 

These changes seem to be working fine in any browser when opening the URL, but when the URL is entered in the Velocity Browser the system throws an error "Sorry, the app could not be opened" . Is Ivanti Velocity Web Browser supported for screen personas using Public Cloud? 

 

Thanks for all your help!

entry.title: Invoice summery can not be sent to customer via e-mail
entry.content: On output items, when I selected the invoice summery and push the send bottom, the system said that it does not support output channel "00010."

entry.title: "User has no authorization for operation 01 on object BUS3008" when using API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV
entry.content:
We are trying to upload attachments (PDF files) to Vendor (Supplier) master data using the standard SAP OData service: API: API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV Communication Scenario: SAP_COM_0008 but it fails with

"User has no authorization for operation 01 on object BUS3008"

 

Why ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Incorrect Exchange rates for Calculated costs
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are using make to order scenario (MTO) and when the sales order is created and process order is processed and when we carryout the costing at SO level, the Calculated cost that is shown on the sales order picks up the incorrect exchange rate as compared to the exchange rate on the order (or other condition types). 

Can you help us understand how is this exchange rate picked up on the condition type PC02-Calculated costs.

entry.title: Storage Location not mandatory in PO
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

Description:
We have a requirement to make Storage Location mandatory in Purchase Orders.
Tried using Field Selection Key (NBF) in document type configuration, but the field remains optional.

Please confirm:

  • Is this possible via standard configuration in S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  • If not, what is the recommended SAP approach?

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Alternative to Document Type ER for Intercompany Travel Expense Recharge
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and using the following scope items for cross-company cost allocation and intercompany billing processes:

  • 4AN – Intercompany Billing for Cross-Company Cost Accounting Postings
  • 4AU – Project Control Intercompany
  • 16T – Intercompany Processes – Project-Based Services

During our analysis of the standard process for cross-company cost recharging (especially travel expense recharges), we noticed that the SAP Best Practice documentation for scope item 4AU uses document type ER – Manual Travel Expenses when posting intercompany expenses.

In the process step "Post Cross-Company Expenses", a supplier invoice is created using document type ER and posted to a WBS element before the subsequent intercompany billing process is executed.

Our Challenge

In our system landscape, travel expenses and other employee-related costs originate from upstream systems and are transferred to SAP via interfaces (e.g. Travel & Expense solutions).

As a result, users do not manually create supplier invoices in SAP using document type ER, as described in the Best Practice process.

When we use document type ER, we encounter problems with currency conversion as well as with advance payment invoices

Instead, the costs already exist in SAP as FI postings generated through interfaces.

Questions

We would like to understand what SAP-recommended alternatives exist for enabling intercompany travel expense recharges and other intercompany cost reallocations when document type ER cannot be used.

Specifically:

  1. Is the use of document type ER a technical prerequisite for generating the subsequent intercompany billing request in scope items 4AN / 4AU?
  2. We would very much like to use the SAP standard and implement a fully automated process
  3. Are there SAP Best Practices for intercompany travel expense recharges when the original costs are transferred from external expense management systems?
  4. Does anyone have experience implementing scope items 4AN, 4AU, and 16T in scenarios where travel expenses originate outside SAP and are integrated via interfaces?

Target Scenario

Our objective is to use the standard SAP intercompany billing functionality without requiring manual reposting of travel expenses through supplier invoices with document type ER.

We would appreciate any guidance, SAP Notes, Best Practices, or project experiences regarding this topic.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Journal entries with 0 interest amount are not displayed in "Manage Interest Calculation Runs" app a
entry.content:
[Component / Application]

  • Job: Schedule Interest Calculation Jobs

  • App: Manage Interest Calculation Runs

[Symptom / Actual Behavior] When executing the interest calculation via the "Schedule Interest Calculation Jobs" app, the source journal entry numbers are normally output and can be verified in the "Manage Interest Calculation Runs" app. However, for journal entries where the interest calculation process was executed but the resulting calculated interest amount was "0", they are not output or displayed in the "Manage Interest Calculation Runs" app.

[Questions]

  1. Is it the standard behavior (intended specification) of SAP S/4HANA Cloud that journal entries with a calculated interest amount of 0 are not displayed in the "Manage Interest Calculation Runs" app?

  2. If it is standard behavior, is there any configuration change or parameter setting available to output/display these journal entries with 0 interest amount in the app?

entry.title: Free Goods YA00 Access Sequence in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently working on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and need guidance regarding the configuration possibilities for the standard free goods condition type YA00.

Business Requirement:

Our customer requires additional determination criteria for free goods processing. During our analysis, we identified that the standard condition type YA00 is used for free goods determination; however, we have not found a configuration option that allows us to maintain, modify, or extend the access sequence assigned to this condition type. 

Currently, the standard accesses used by condition YA00 appear to be based on the following combinations: 

  1. Customer / Material 
  2. Sales Organization / Distribution Channel / Customer Pricing Group / Material 
  3. Sales Organization / Distribution Channel / Material 

Questions 

We would like to understand the standard capabilities provided by SAP in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition regarding free goods determination and condition YA00: 

  1. Is it possible to modify the standard access sequence used by condition type YA00? 
  2. Is it possible to create new access sequences for free goods determination? 
  3. Is there any SSCUI, CBC configuration activity, expert configuration, or extensibility option that allows customers to define additional access combinations beyond the standard ones delivered by SAP? 
  4. If modification of the standard access sequence is not supported, what alternative approaches are recommended by SAP to fulfill customer-specific free goods determination requirements? 

Expected Outcome 

We would appreciate confirmation of the supported standard configuration options and any SAP-recommended alternatives that would allow us to implement additional determination criteria for free goods processing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

Thank you for your support, 
María Guzmán.

entry.title: 我在银行清账时显示没有容差组，问题该怎么解决
entry.content: 在银行清账时出现提示“没有容差组”，我想知道如何解决这个问题。对该问题的解决方案我需要确认相关配置和设置，确保容差组正确维护和分配。如果需要，我希望通过支持案例进行详细排查。

entry.title: Bypass of standard error message
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

We want to do mass closure of some specific POs, but due to some standard errors, we are not able to close them.

MEPO -254Acceptance at Origin cannot be used with AccAsgt categories with no stock 
ME-347Release orders against contract 4610068068 not possible (contract not released) 

Is there any way we can bypass those standard errors? 

Regards,

Palak

entry.title: DRC for India GSTR reports
entry.content:
Need to Activate DRC for GSTR report with additional feteaures


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Link list in capacity scheduling table
entry.content:
Hi all,

in the capacity scheduling table, you cannot directly modify the production order or planned orders. The link list only displays these items. Are there any ways to expand this link list further (change planned order; change production order)?

 

Kind regards,

Jana

entry.title: Customer project - credit management on sales order
entry.content:
Dear SAP experts,

We want to apply credit management on customer project, and more precisely on sales order created for the project.
We found this note indicating that it is possible to apply credit management on sales order type Sales Order Service (SO01) https://me.sap.com/notes/3406324/E, however solution does not work as we still get the error message ""You cannot process a sales order of type SO01 (customer project)".

We tested the credit control functionality using a standard order for the exact same customer, and it worked perfectly. Moreover, if we follow all the project steps through to invoice generation, the invoice will correctly impact the credit limit.

Could you please help us identify where the issue comes from please ?

Best regards

entry.title: CDS view Product classes and characterstics
entry.content: is it just us or do more companies have the opinion SAP is consistently inconsistent with what they turn on for Data Extraction. we are now after characterics and classes for Products/Material which is pretty critical for us. FLOC and Equipment CDS views are turned on but these are not. Why not? when they are? how do other cope with this??

entry.title: CAMT53 into API structure for communication arrangement : SAP_COM_0316 ( NO MBC)
entry.content:
We are in the process of implementing the incoming CAMT53 statement from Bank. In this regard, we understand that there is provision in MBC. But, without MBC, we are using SAP_COM_0316 communication arrangement. However, what would be best way to map the incoming CAMT53 with API Structure. I tried to map the XML with API structure, but not able to find the transaction details int he API structure.

I understand that one check the missing tag in XML by uploading the same in the Test Incoming Format Mapping :

entry.title: Badi for the Stock Information message at PR level
entry.content:
Dear Team,

The client has a requirement to display the available stock of a material as an informational message at two stages:

  1. During Purchase Requisition (PR) creation
  2. At the My Inbox (approver) level

Is there any body where we can make this customization by using BADI.

entry.title: Projecting WBS Element onto Customer Line Items
entry.content:
We are currently unable to view the WBS Element on Customer Line Items within standard operational apps, and we need guidance on the standard configuration to enable this.

During a customer invoice posting (e.g., Journal Entry 9400000112), the WBS Element is successfully populated on the P&L line item (Item 000002 shows WBS 'UAE/ACC/23/WO' in I_ACCOUNTINGDOCUMENTJOURNAL). However, the Customer/AR reconciliation line (Item 000001) leaves the WBS Element field blank. Consequently, the WBS Element does not appear when viewing the invoice in the "Manage Customer Line Items" app

entry.title: Public Cloud - How to post a prior-year direct supplier invoice for an asset when MM period is close
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, our client forgot to post a fixed asset purchased in June 2025. The asset master is already created, and now they want to record the acquisition with the original posting date (June 2025).

When we use Create Supplier Invoice or Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced (direct invoice without PO), we receive Message M8022 stating that the MM posting period is not allowed, even though the FI posting period is open.

The Post Asset Acquisition app allows the posting, but it does not create the supplier liability.

Is there any standard way in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to post a prior-year direct supplier invoice for an asset (Dr Asset / Cr Supplier), or is Post Asset Acquisition the only supported option in this scenario?

Thank you in advance for your guidance.
